news.Title: Is Micron the Next Nvidia?
news.Content: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) is having a moment. The maker of memory chips just posted second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year over year. Gross margins have scaled up past 70%, a figure that would have seemed absurd for a memory company just three years ago. The stock trades at a forward P/E below 10, while the semiconductor industry median is around 30. If you squint, it ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) reported a significant Q2 FY2026 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year-over-year, with gross margins exceeding 70%.
• The company's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) is critical for AI accelerators, with capacity sold out through 2026 and shortages expected next year.
• Micron's return on equity is 40%, and its addressable market could reach $100 billion by 2028, positioning it as a compelling 'pick-and-shovel' play in the AI ecosystem.
• Unlike Nvidia, which designs AI accelerators and controls the software ecosystem, Micron is a component supplier vulnerable to price competition from peers like Samsung and SK Hynix.
• The stock trades at a forward P/E below 10, significantly lower than the semiconductor industry median of around 30, reflecting market concerns about the cyclical nature of the memory market and long-term pricing power.
news.Keywords: memory chips, high-bandwidth memory, AI supply chain, gross margin, valuation discount

news.Title: What Is the Cheapest "Magnificent Seven" Stock?
news.Content: The "Magnificent Seven" group of stocks includes some of the largest companies in the world, all of which are major players in the tech realm. This group is made up of: Nvidia Alphabet Apple Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) Amazon Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) Tesla All seven of these stocks are trillion-dollar companies by market cap. However, each stock varies in how good a deal ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The 'Magnificent Seven' stocks (Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta Platforms, Tesla) are all trillion-dollar companies, but their valuations vary significantly.
• Operating cash flow is identified as the most reliable valuation metric for these companies due to significant capital expenditures and investment gains affecting net income.
• Tesla (95x CFO) and Nvidia (56x CFO) are the most expensive, though Nvidia's strong growth is expected to reduce its multiple.
• Meta Platforms (13x CFO) appears to be the cheapest, trading at a historically low multiple, potentially offering a buying opportunity.
• Microsoft (less than 18x CFO) is also highlighted as a potential value play, trading below its historical average despite strong business performance.
news.Keywords: Magnificent Seven, valuation metrics, operating cash flow, relative valuation, value investing

news.Title: AMD buys $6.5 million of surging tech stock
news.Content: Advanced Micro Devices ( AMD ) has gained about 98% year to date, according to Yahoo Finance at the time of writing, Sunday morning, May 17. Meanwhile, the SPDR S&P 500 index ( SPY ) is up about 8.4% in the same period. The company has outpaced the S&P 500 by a huge margin. That is impressive, but what is driving these gains? AMD is a semiconductor company that makes CPUs and GPUs, and its stock has rallied amid the AI boom. Key news items for AMD stock: At ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has seen its stock rally nearly 98% year-to-date, significantly outperforming the S&P 500, driven by the AI boom.
• AMD Ventures, the company's corporate venture capital arm, invested $6.5 million in Marvell (MRVL) stock at approximately $99.05 per share on March 31st.
• This investment in Marvell, a key semiconductor player for data center networking, likely followed Nvidia's strategic partnership and $2 billion investment in the company.
• The investment is seen as a strong signal of confidence, particularly given Marvell's role in silicon photonics, a technology expected to be crucial for future networking infrastructure.
• Despite a recent dip in semiconductor stocks due to geopolitical factors and analyst warnings, the underlying trend of hyperscaler investment in AI buildout suggests continued rally potential.
news.Keywords: venture investment, semiconductor sector, AI buildout, silicon photonics, strategic partnership

news.Title: The AI Stock With a 10-Year Head Start That Wall Street Still Hasn't Fully Priced In
news.Content: As the expenses of building and running artificial intelligence (AI) data centers continue to surge, having lower costs is a huge advantage. One of the best ways to achieve this is with custom chips called application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). ASICs for AI are hardwired chips developed for specific tasks. Not ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOGL) has a significant advantage in AI due to its decade-long development of custom Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), which are more cost-effective and energy-efficient than competitors' GPUs.
• The company has optimized its hardware and software stack around its TPUs, leading to lower costs for training AI models and running inference.
• Alphabet's eighth-generation TPUs offer specialized variations for training (TPU 8t) and inference/agentic AI (TPU 8i), paired with custom Arm-based CPUs.
• The company is beginning to allow select customers to purchase TPUs directly, presenting a new growth avenue.
• Despite its AI leadership and custom silicon, Alphabet's stock is considered undervalued with a forward P/E of 28 on 2026 estimates.
news.Keywords: custom silicon, AI infrastructure, cost advantage, inference, training

news.Title: Snapchat: Don't Rush to Buy This Social Media Stock
news.Content: It's been a tough ride for long-term Snap (NYSE: SNAP) investors. Once touted as a close rival to Meta Platforms ' Instagram, the relatively small social media company remains unprofitable and is down by more than 30% year to date. Investors who are betting on a turnaround ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Snap (SNAP) has struggled, remaining unprofitable and down over 30% year-to-date, making it a less attractive investment compared to Meta Platforms (META).
• Snap's revenue growth rate of 8.8% over the past three years significantly lags Meta's 19.9% CAGR.
• With less than $10 billion in revenue compared to Meta's over $200 billion, Snap faces intense competition from platforms like Instagram and TikTok.
• Despite a 5% year-over-year increase in monthly active users (956 million), Snap needs to improve average revenue per user, which has shown limited progress.
• While Q2 revenue is projected to grow 14.6% year-over-year to $1.535 billion, sequential growth is expected to be flat, indicating persistent growth challenges.
news.Keywords: unprofitable, revenue growth, market share, user monetization, competitive landscape

news.Title: Microsoft Stock Is an AI Bargain That Investors Are Missing
news.Content: Despite dominating the cloud computing and artificial intelligence (AI) space, Microsoft (MSFT) stock has gone nowhere. Over the past year, shares have fallen roughly 9.6%, lagging rivals such as Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), whose stock has surged on AI enthusiasm, and Amazon (AMZN) , which has gained 28%. More News from Barchart Intel Is Supposed to Be in a New CPU Era But Its Losing Market Share to AMD and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) stock has underperformed rivals like Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN), despite its strong position in cloud and AI.

news.Title: Micron's and Sandisk's Futures Are Heavily Influenced by 2 Foreign Chip Companies. Here's How to Buy Them.
news.Content: Demand for memory chips has skyrocketed over the past year as big tech companies upped their spending on artificial intelligence (AI) data centers. That's been a huge boon for both Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) and Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) , which have benefited from tremendous pricing power. Earnings at both companies have soared, as have their stock prices. Micron shares are up over 700% in the past 12 months, and Sandisk stock is up a whopping 3,200%. But investors eager to hop on the bandwagon need to be aware ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Demand for memory chips has surged due to AI data center spending, benefiting Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK), with stock gains of over 700% and 3,200% respectively in the past year.
• The memory chip market is heavily influenced by Korean companies Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, which dominate DRAM and NAND markets and lead in High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM).
• These two companies' production capacity decisions significantly impact industry pricing and earnings cycles.
• Samsung is increasing investments in memory chip production, while SK Hynix is expanding existing facilities and collaborating on new ones.
• A potential global production build-out led by Samsung and SK Hynix could negatively impact pricing by 2028, posing a risk to Micron and Sandisk if they cannot offset lower prices with volume.
news.Keywords: memory chips, AI data centers, supply/demand, pricing power, production capacity

news.Title: Where are Amazon’s headquarters? Seattle and beyond
news.Content: Jef Bezos and his wife, MacKenzie Scott, drove from New York City to Seattle to start Amazon.com in a rented garage. They stayed there and made Washington’s biggest city the headquarters for the world’s biggest online retailer. Later on, the company grew, and as part of its expansion, it set up a second headquarters on the East Coast. Here’s where Amazon’s two different headquarters are located and how many people work at each one. Where is Amazon’s headquarters? The area of Amazon’s first and original headquarters, which it ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon's original headquarters are located in Seattle, Washington, spread across the Puget Sound area.
• The company established a second headquarters, known as HQ2 or Metropolitan Park, in Arlington, Virginia, to accommodate its growing workforce.
• The Seattle headquarters houses over 45,000 workers, while the Arlington location can accommodate up to 14,000 employees.
• The article details the physical attributes and amenities of both headquarters, including office buildings, retail spaces, and employee facilities.
news.Keywords: headquarters, expansion, corporate locations, workforce

news.Title: Why Everyone Is Talking About Lumentum Stock -- and Why Investors Should Care
news.Content: For much of the artificial intelligence (AI) boom, investors focused on the companies building the brains behind AI. news.Title: TSMC predicts semiconductor market will reach $1.5 trillion by 2030
news.Content: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company ( TSM ) is up 40% in 2026 alone as investors price in a much larger chip market and a deeper role for the company in its most profitable layers. As demand for AI accelerates, TSMC just reported that it now sees the semiconductor market reaching $1.5 trillion by 2030. The company now sits at the center of nearly every major AI buildout because the world’s most advanced AI chips cannot be produced at scale without TSMC’s manufacturing and packaging technology. TSMC redraws the 2030 chip map In TSMC’s latest long-range outlook, management now sees the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) predicts the semiconductor market will reach $1.5 trillion by 2030, with 55% driven by AI and high-performance computing.

• The company's Q1 2026 results showed strong revenue growth of 40.6% year-over-year and a gross margin of 66.2%, exceeding expectations.
• TSMC raised its full-year 2026 outlook to over 30% revenue growth, driven by strong AI demand and its control over critical manufacturing bottlenecks like CoWoS packaging, which is expected to remain tight through at least 2027.

news.Title: POET Stock Jumped on $500 Million Deal With Lumilens. Its Financials Have a Long Way to Go.

Yet, when Poet Technologies (POET) revealed that it had signed a $500 million deal with photonics-focused company, Lumilens, there must have been some in the market who believed that there could be a correction in the stock. However, there was no repeat of the Marvell (MRVL) episode, and Poet shares ended yesterday's trading session up a whopping 43%. news.AI_Summary:

• The partnership combines Poet's photonic integration platforms with Lumilens's optical chipsets and manufacturing capabilities.

• Poet's Optical Interposer technology aims to reduce cost, size, and power consumption for optical interconnects in AI infrastructure.

news.Title: Is Silicon Motion Technology the Next Sandisk?
news.Content: Memory stocks have boomed during the past year as more investors recognize the connection between AI chips and memory chips. No stock seems to be as hot as Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) , which is up by more than 3,000% during that time. It's also up by almost 500% year to date, so it's natural that some investors are looking for a smaller version of the company. Silicon Motion Technology (NASDAQ: SIMO) may be the answer. It's a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO) produces NAND flash controllers, essential for NAND flash chips used in AI models.
• SIMO reported 23% sequential revenue growth in Q1 and expects high sequential growth for the rest of the year.
• The company's Q1 revenue was $342.1 million, exceeding its own forecast of $306 million.
• SIMO shares have nearly doubled since its Q1 results and tripled year-to-date.
• The article highlights the cyclical nature of semiconductor stocks but suggests AI demand may mitigate this for SIMO.
news.Keywords: NAND controllers, AI exposure, sequential revenue growth, Q1 earnings, market catalysts

news.Title: Cerebras Raises $5.55 Billion IPO at $40 Billion Valuation
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Cerebras Systems could be emerging as one of the most closely watched artificial intelligence infrastructure listings of the year after the chipmaker raised $5.55 billion in its US initial ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cerebras Systems raised $5.55 billion in its US IPO at $185 per share, valuing the company at $40 billion (fully diluted $49 billion).
• Investor demand for AI semiconductor exposure was exceptionally strong, with orders exceeding available shares by over 20 times.
• Cerebras has established relationships with major AI players like Amazon and OpenAI.
• The IPO is the largest US listing this year, with Arm Holdings (ARM) and SoftBank reportedly exploring an acquisition.
• Cerebras reported net income of $87.9 million on revenue of $510 million for 2025, a significant improvement from a net loss in the prior year.
news.Keywords: IPO, AI infrastructure, valuation, investor demand, strategic partnerships

news.Title: Analog Devices (ADI): The Best Electric Vehicle Supply Chain Stock to Invest In

• ADI is considered a top Electric Vehicle Supply Chain Stock, though some AI stocks may offer greater upside potential.

主 旨：世芯-KY董事會決議不 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 世芯-KY董事會決議不繼續辦理原於114年股東常會通過的私募普通股案。
• 公司原計畫於股東會決議日起一年內，辦理不超過1,500,000股的私募。
• 第一次私募後剩餘額度942,675股，因無繼續辦理計畫，故不繼續辦理。
• 此案將提報115年度股東常會報告。

news.Title: 《韓股》KOSPI創收盤新高 半導體/汽車股突出
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-13 16:23:38 數位內容中心 發佈

韓股今(13)日呈現開低走高。韓國KOSPI指 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 韓國KOSPI指數終場收在7844.01點，上漲2.63%，創下收盤歷史新高。
• 半導體與汽車族群為推升主力，SK Hynix漲7.68%，Hyundai Motor漲9.91%。
• 美國CPI數據引發早盤全球科技與半導體股承壓，但隨後買盤回補帶動指數上漲。
• 提及比亞迪4月海外銷量創高，以及川普可能對歐盟汽車關稅加徵至25%。
news.Keywords: KOSPI指數,創歷史新高,半導體股,汽車股,CPI數據

news.Title: 【公告】世芯-KY董事會決議114年第1次私募普通股定價及應募人資訊
news.Content:
日 期：2026年05月13日

公司名稱：世芯-KY (3661)

主 旨：世芯-KY董事會決議114年第1次私募普通股定價及應募人資訊

發言人：王德善

說 明：

1.董事會決議日期:115/05/13

2.私募有價證券種類:普通股

3.私募對象及其與公 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 世芯-KY董事會決議114年第1次私募普通股定價為每股4,239元，應募人為Amazon.com, Inc.，認購股數為557,325股。
• 本次私募資金將用於充實公司長期營運發展所需之營運週轉金。
• 私募價格訂定參考價為4,987元，實際私募價格為參考價的85%，符合股東會決議最低成數。
• 私募普通股於交付日起三年內不得自由轉讓，三年後依規定申請上市交易。
news.Keywords: 私募定價,策略性投資人,營運週轉金,現金增資,股權稀釋
